name: Bandwidth
what_it_does: Carrier-grade communications API platform owned by Bandwidth Inc., offering voice, messaging, and 911 services with direct carrier network access for building enterprise telephony applications.
use_cases: Building enterprise IVR and voice automation workflows with carrier-grade reliability
Sending high-volume SMS/MMS messaging with direct carrier network routing
Implementing 911 (emergency services) API for location-based routing in safety applications
Provisioning and managing phone numbers programmatically for multi-tenant applications
WebRTC voice application integration with PSTN connectivity
not_for: Simple SMS notifications where Twilio or Sinch are easier to implement
Non-US voice/messaging (Bandwidth focuses primarily on US market)
Small-scale developers without significant call/message volume (pricing structured for enterprise)
In-app chat messaging (Bandwidth is carrier-network focused, not app messaging)
best_when: You're building enterprise or carrier-grade voice/messaging applications in the US and need direct carrier network access, 911 capabilities, or very high-volume messaging.
avoid_when: You need global coverage, simple SMS workflows, or are a small team without significant volume — Twilio or Sinch are simpler to get started with.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using API username and password (API token and API secret). Account ID required as part of API path. Separate credentials for messaging vs voice APIs.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-minute voice, per-message SMS pricing; enterprise contracts available
pricing.notes: No self-service trial — requires account setup with Bandwidth. Pricing is competitive for enterprise volume but not optimized for small developers.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by account type; enterprise accounts have high throughput limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes with XML and JSON error formats (mixed across endpoints). Error messages are functional but documentation can be inconsistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Call creation is not idempotent. Message sending is not idempotent. Number provisioning operations are generally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Some Bandwidth APIs return XML, not JSON — agents must handle both content types or use SDK
Account ID is part of the URL path, not a header — every API call requires the account ID
Voice and messaging APIs have different base URLs and auth contexts
Call control events are delivered via webhook callbacks — agents must expose a public HTTPS endpoint
BXML (Bandwidth XML) is required for voice call flow control — proprietary markup language to learn

security_score_components.security_notes: Telephony API. Basic auth (username/password) for API calls. Webhook validation via HMAC. Phone numbers and call data are sensitive — CPNI compliance required.
